entities.name: Aberrant Anatomy
entries: Exposure to alien horrors like those of the Far Realm has warped your physical form in supernatural ways. You gain the following features.

entries.name: Breathless
entries: You can hold your breath for 1 hour.

entries.name: Extrasensory Perception
entries: You have proficiency in the {@skill Perception|XPHB} skill, if you lack it. You also gain {@variantrule Expertise|XPHB} in that skill.
In addition, you have {@sense Blindsight|XPHB} with a range of 15 feet.

entries.name: Warping Flesh
entries: Immediately after you make a {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB} and roll a 1 on the {@dice d20}, the aberrant influence infecting your form flares, wrenching control of your flesh. Make a Constitution saving throw (DC 13 plus your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}). On a failed save, you have the {@condition Stunned|XPHB} condition until the end of your next turn.

entities.name: Aberrant Dragonmark
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Aberrant Fortitude
entries: When you fail a Constitution saving throw, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to roll {@dice 1d4} and add the number rolled to the save, potentially turning the failure into a success. Once you've used this benefit, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Aberrant Magic
entries: You know one {@filter cantrip of your choice from the Sorcerer spell list|spells|level=0|class=Sorcerer}. Also, choose a level 1 spell from that spell list. You always have that spell prepared. You can cast it once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it in that way when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also cast this spell using any spell slots you have. Constitution is your spellcasting ability for this spell.

entries.name: Aberrant Surge
entries: When you cast the level 1 spell from this feat, you can expend one of your {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB} and roll it. If you roll an even number, you gain a number of {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the number rolled. If you roll an odd number, one creature within 30 feet of you (not including you) takes Force damage equal to the number rolled. If no other creatures are in range, you take the damage.

entities.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: Increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. This feat can't increase an ability score above 20.

entries.name: Repeatable
entries: You can take this feat more than once.

entities.name: Actor
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Impersonation
entries: While you're disguised as a real or fictional person, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Charisma ({@skill Deception|XPHB} or {@skill Performance|XPHB}) checks to convince others that you are that person.

entries.name: Mimicry
entries: You can mimic the sounds of other creatures, including speech. A creature that hears the mimicry must succeed on a Wisdom ({@skill Insight|XPHB}) check to determine the effect is faked ({@dc 8} plus your Charisma modifier and {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}).

entities.name: Alert
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Initiative Proficiency
entries: When you roll {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB}, you can add your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} to the roll.

entries.name: Initiative Swap
entries: Immediately after you roll {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB}, you can swap your {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} with the {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} of one willing ally in the same combat. You can't make this swap if you or the ally has the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition.

entities.name: Archery
entries: You gain a +2 bonus to attack rolls you make with Ranged weapons.

entities.name: Athlete
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Climb Speed
entries: You gain a {@variantrule Climb Speed|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}.

entries.name: Hop Up
entries: When you have the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition, you can right yourself with only 5 feet of movement.

entries.name: Jumping
entries: You can make a running {@variantrule Long Jump|XPHB|Long} or {@variantrule High Jump|XPHB} after moving only 5 feet.

entities.name: Blessed Warrior

otherSummary.entry: When Gaining the Level 2 Paladin "Fighting Style" Feature

entries: You learn two {@filter Cleric cantrips|spells|level=0|class=Cleric} of your choice. {@spell Guidance|XPHB} and {@spell Sacred Flame|XPHB} are recommended. The chosen cantrips count as Paladin spells for you, and Charisma is your spellcasting ability for them. Whenever you gain a Paladin level, you can replace one of these cantrips with another Cleric cantrip.

entities.name: Blind Fighting
entries: You have {@sense Blindsight|XPHB} with a range of 10 feet.

entities.name: Bloodlust
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Powerful Recovery
entries: When you roll a {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB|Hit Point Die} to regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, you can treat any roll of 1 or 2 as a 3.

entries.name: Sanguine Feast
entries: Once per turn when you hit a {@status Bloodied|XPHB} creature that isn't a Construct or Undead with an attack roll, you can expend a {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB|Hit Point Die}, roll it, and regain a number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the number rolled plus your Constitution modifier.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Bomber
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Far Lobber
entries: When you use the {@action Attack|XPHB} action to throw a vial or flask, you can target an object or creature you can see within 40 feet of yourself.

entries.name: Long Shots
entries: Attacking at long range doesn't impose {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on your attack rolls with {@itemProperty T|XPHB|Thrown} weapons.

entities.name: Boon of Blazing Dawn
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Beloved of the Sun
entries: You have {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to Radiant damage.

entries.name: Blazing Strike
entries: When you hit a creature with a weapon attack, the damage can be Radiant or the weapon's normal damage type (your choice).

entries.name: Burst of Sunlight
entries: Once per turn when you hit a creature with an attack that deals Radiant damage, you can emit {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} in a 30-foot radius from yourself and {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} for an additional 30 feet until the start of your next turn. This light is sunlight.

entities.name: Boon of Bloodshed
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Killer's Fortune
entries: When an enemy you can see is reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, you gain {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the next attack roll you make before the end of your next turn.

entries.name: Power from Pain
entries: Once per turn, when you make an attack roll while {@status Bloodied|XPHB}, you can deal extra damage to the target equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}. The extra damage's type is the same as the attack's type.

entities.name: Boon of Bountiful Health
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Augmented Health
entries: When you gain {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB}, increase the number of {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} you gain by 5.

entries.name: Superior Recuperation
entries: When you spend one or more {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB} to regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, you can instead use the highest number possible for each die.

entities.name: Boon of Combat Prowess
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Peerless Aim
entries: When you miss with an attack roll, you can hit instead. Once you use this benefit, you can't use it again until the start of your next turn.

entities.name: Boon of Communication
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Cunning Speaker
entries: You don't have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on ability checks to influence {@variantrule Hostile [Attitude]|XPHB|Hostile} creatures.

entries.name: Gifted Interpreter
entries: You understand the literal meaning of any language you hear or see signed, and you can understand the literal meaning of any written language you see.

entries.name: Mental Communication
entries: You gain telepathy with a range of 120 feet.

entities.name: Boon of Desperate Resilience
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Defense of Body and Mind
entries: While you are {@status Bloodied|XPHB}, you have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to every damage type except Force.

entities.name: Boon of Dimensional Travel
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Blink Steps
entries: Immediately after you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action or the {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see.

entities.name: Boon of Energy Resistance
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Energy Resistances
entries: You gain {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to two of the following damage types of your choice: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, Necrotic, Poison, Psychic, Radiant, or Thunder. Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can change your choices.

entries.name: Energy Redirection
entries: When you take damage of one of the types chosen for the Energy Resistances benefit, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to direct damage of the same type toward another creature you can see within 60 feet of yourself that isn't behind {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Total Cover}. If you do so, that creature must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw ({@dc 8} plus your Constitution modifier and {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}) or take damage equal to {@dice 2d12} plus your Constitution modifier.

entities.name: Boon of Exquisite Radiance
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Eternal Rest
entries: Creatures you reduce to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} can't become Undead.

entries.name: Powerful Radiance
entries: When you make a damage roll that deals Radiant damage, you can instead use the highest number possible for each damage die. Once you use this benefit, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Boon of Fate
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Improve Fate
entries: When you or another creature within 60 feet of you succeeds on or fails a {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB}, you can roll {@dice 2d4} and apply the total rolled as a bonus or penalty to the {@dice d20} roll. Once you use this benefit, you can't use it again until you roll {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} or finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Boon of Fluid Forms
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Shapechanger
entries: You can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to shape-shift into a Beast, Humanoid, or Monstrosity with a {@variantrule Challenge Rating|XPHB} no higher than 10. When you shape-shift, you gain a number of {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} of the form. The shape-shifting effect lasts for 1 hour, and it ends early if you have no {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} left or if you take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to return to your true form.

Your game statistics are replaced by the stat block of the chosen form, but you retain your creature type; alignment; personality; Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma scores; {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}; {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB}; proficiencies; and ability to communicate. If you have the Spellcasting or Pact Magic feature, you retain it too. Upon shape-shifting, you determine whether your equipment drops to the ground or changes in size and shape to fit the new form while you're in it.
Once you use this benefit, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Hardy Transformation
entries: When you gain {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} when you shape-shift, increase that number of {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} by 20.

entities.name: Boon of Fortitude
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Fortified Health
entries: Your {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} maximum increases by 40. In addition, whenever you regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, you can regain additional {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to your Constitution modifier. Once you've regained these additional {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, you can't do so again until the start of your next turn.

entities.name: Boon of Fortune's Favor
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Saving Throw Reroll
entries: When you fail a saving throw, you can reroll it and must use the new roll. Once you use this benefit, you can't do so again until the start of your next turn.

entities.name: Boon of Irresistible Offense
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Overcome Defenses
entries: The Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage you deal always ignores {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB}.

entries.name: Overwhelming Strike
entries: When you roll a 20 on the {@dice d20} for an attack roll, you can deal extra damage to the target equal to the ability score increased by this feat. The extra damage's type is the same as the attack's type.

entities.name: Boon of Looming Shadows
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Shadowy Stretch
entries: When you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action, your reach for your Melee weapon attacks increases by 10 feet until the end of your turn.

entries.name: Dancing Silhouette
entries: You can take the {@action Dodge|XPHB} action as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}.

entities.name: Boon of Misty Escape
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Gaseous Form
entries: If you drop to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} but aren't killed outright, you can instead drop to 1 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} and cast {@spell Gaseous Form|XPHB} without expending a spell slot (no action required). When you cast this spell this way, you can target only yourself, your {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} is 20 feet, and you regain 10 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} at the start of each of your turns for the spell's duration. The spell's spellcasting ability is the ability increased by this feat.
Once you use this benefit, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Boon of Poison Mastery
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Antitoxic
entries: You have {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to Poison damage and the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition.

entries.name: Perfect Poisoner
entries: Once per turn, when you roll dice to determine Poison damage a creature takes from your attack, spell, or feature, you can instead use the highest number possible for each die.

entities.name: Boon of Recovery
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Last Stand
entries: When you would be reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, you can drop to 1 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} instead and regain a number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to half your {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} maximum. Once you use this benefit, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Recover Vitality
entries: You have a pool of ten d10s. As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can expend dice from the pool, roll those dice, and regain a number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the roll's total. You regain all the expended dice when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Boon of Revelry
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Inspire Dance
entries: You always have the {@spell Otto's Irresistible Dance|XPHB} spell prepared. You can cast it once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it that way when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also cast the spell using any spell slots you have of the appropriate level.
When you cast the spell, it requires no spell components, and taking damage doesn't break your {@status Concentration|XPHB} on it.

entries.name: Sing Out
entries: While a creature that failed its saving throw against your {@spell Otto's Irresistible Dance|XPHB} has the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} condition from that spell, it can't cast spells with Verbal components, and it sings delightful nonsense if it can sing.

entities.name: Boon of Siberys
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Aberrant Magic
entries: Choose a level 8 or lower spell from the {@filter Sorcerer spell list|spells|class=Sorcerer|level=0;1;2;3;4;5;6;7;8} or a spell from the Siberys Dragonmark Spells table (the table includes dragonmark suggestions if you'd like to associate a spell with one of the marks). You always have that spell prepared. You can cast it once without a spell slot or spell components, and you regain the ability to cast it in that way when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also cast this spell using any spell slots you have of the appropriate level. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for this spell (choose when you gain this feat).

entities.name: Boon of Skill
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: All-Around Adept
entries: You gain proficiency in all skills.

entries.name: Expertise
entries: Choose one skill in which you lack {@variantrule Expertise|XPHB}. You gain {@variantrule Expertise|XPHB} in that skill.

entities.name: Boon of Speed
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Escape Artist
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can take the {@action Disengage|XPHB} action, which also ends the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition on you.

entries.name: Quickness
entries: Your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} increases by 30 feet.

entities.name: Boon of Spell Recall
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Free Casting
entries: Whenever you cast a spell with a level 1-4 spell slot, roll {@dice 1d4}. If the number you roll is the same as the slot's level, the slot isn't expended.

entities.name: Boon of Terror
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Fearless
entries: You have {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition.

entries.name: Flee, Fools!
entries: When a creature with the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition starts its turn within 60 feet of you, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to stoke its terror, provided you can see the creature and it isn't behind {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Total Cover}. If you do so, the creature must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw (DC 8 plus your Charisma modifier and your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}) or spend its turn moving away from you by the fastest available means. Once you use this benefit, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Intimidating
entries: You gain {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB} in the {@skill Intimidation|XPHB} skill if you don't already have it. You also gain {@variantrule Expertise|XPHB} in {@skill Intimidation|XPHB}.

entities.name: Boon of the Bright Sun
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Daylight Presence
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you radiate a 30-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} of {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} that is sunlight. If any of the {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation}'s area overlaps with an area of {@spell Darkness|XPHB} created by a spell, that spell is dispelled. The {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} lasts until you dismiss it (no action required), die, or have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition.

entries.name: Fortifying Light
entries: When your Daylight Presence is active, at the start of each of your turns, you and allies you can see in your Daylight Presence gain 10 {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB}.

entities.name: Boon of the Furious Storm
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Eye of the Storm
entries: You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Lightning and Thunder damage. While you are {@status Bloodied|XPHB}, you have {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to Lightning and Thunder damage.

entries.name: Storm's Strength
entries: Creatures have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on saving throws against your spells that deal Lightning or Thunder damage.

entities.name: Boon of the Night Spirit
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Merge with Shadows
entries: While within {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} or {@variantrule Darkness|XPHB}, you can give yourself the {@condition Invisible|XPHB} condition as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. The condition ends on you immediately after you take an action, a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, or a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}.

entries.name: Shadowy Form
entries: While within {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} or {@variantrule Darkness|XPHB}, you have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to all damage except Psychic and Radiant.

entities.name: Boon of the Soul Drinker
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Grave Resistance
entries: You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Cold damage and Necrotic damage.

entries.name: Siphon Life
entries: When an enemy within 120 feet of you is reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to regain 50 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}. Once you use this benefit, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Boon of Truesight
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Truesight
entries: You have {@sense Truesight|XPHB} with a range of 60 feet.

entities.name: Charger
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Improved Dash
entries: When you take the {@action Dash|XPHB} action, your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} increases by 10 feet for that action.

entries.name: Charge Attack
entries: If you move at least 10 feet in a straight line toward a target immediately before hitting it with a melee attack roll as part of the {@action Attack|XPHB} action, choose one of the following effects: gain a {@dice 1d8} bonus to the attack's damage roll, or push the target up to 10 feet away if it is no more than one size larger than you. You can use this benefit only once on each of your turns.

entities.name: Chef
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Cook's Utensils
entries: You gain proficiency with {@item Cook's Utensils|XPHB} if you don't already have it.

entries.name: Replenishing Meal
entries: As part of a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB}, you can cook special food if you have ingredients and {@item Cook's Utensils|XPHB} on hand. You can prepare enough of this food for a number of creatures equal to 4 plus your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}. At the end of the {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB}, any creature who eats the food and spends one or more Hit Dice to regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} regains an extra {@dice 1d8} {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}.

entries.name: Bolstering Treats
entries: With 1 hour of work or when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can cook a number of treats equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} if you have ingredients and {@item Cook's Utensils|XPHB} on hand. These special treats last 8 hours after being made. A creature can use a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to eat one of those treats to gain a number of {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}.

entities.name: Child of the Sun
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Eyes of Eirdu
entries: You and allies within 10 feet of you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws made to avoid or end the {@condition Blinded|XPHB} condition.

entries.name: Faerie Fire
entries: You learn the {@spell Faerie Fire|XPHB} spell. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for this spell (choose when you select this feat). You can cast it once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it in that way when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also cast the spell using any spell slots you have.
When you cast {@spell Faerie Fire|XPHB} without a spell slot using this benefit, taking damage can't break your {@status Concentration|XPHB} on the spell.

entities.name: Cloying Mists
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Arise, Fog
entries: You always have the {@spell Fog Cloud|XPHB} spell prepared. You can cast it without a spell slot, and you must finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} before you can cast it in this way again. You can also cast it using spell slots you have of the appropriate level. Your spellcasting ability for the spell is the ability increased by this feat.

entries.name: Grasping Mist
entries: Whenever you cast {@spell Fog Cloud|XPHB}, nonmagical flames in the spell's {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} are extinguished, and creatures other than you and your allies have their {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} reduced by 5 feet while in the spell's {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere}.

entities.name: Cold Caster
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Cantrip
entries: You learn the {@spell Ray of Frost|XPHB} cantrip. If you already know it, you learn a different Wizard cantrip of your choice. The spell's spellcasting ability is the ability increased by this feat.

entries.name: Frostbite
entries: Once per turn when you hit a creature with an attack roll and deal Cold damage, you can temporarily negate the creature's defenses. The creature subtracts {@dice 1d4} from the next saving throw it makes before the end of your next turn.

entities.name: Crafter
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Tool Proficiency
entries: You gain proficiency with three different {@item Artisan's Tools|XPHB} of your choice from the Fast Crafting table.

entries.name: Discount
entries: Whenever you buy a nonmagical item, you receive a 20 percent discount on it.

entries.name: Fast Crafting
entries: When you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can craft one piece of gear from the Fast Crafting table, provided you have the {@item Artisan's Tools|XPHB} associated with that item and have proficiency with those tools. The item lasts until you finish another {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, at which point the item falls apart.

entries.caption: Fast Crafting
rows: {@item Crossbow Bolt Case|XPHB}, {@item Map or Scroll Case|XPHB}, {@item Pouch|XPHB}
{@item Ball Bearings|XPHB}, {@item Bucket|XPHB}, {@item Caltrops|XPHB}, {@item Grappling Hook|XPHB}, {@item Iron Pot|XPHB}

entities.name: Crossbow Expert
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Ignore Loading
entries: You ignore the {@itemProperty LD|XPHB|Loading} property of the {@item Hand Crossbow|XPHB}, {@item Heavy Crossbow|XPHB}, and {@item Light Crossbow|XPHB} (all called crossbows elsewhere in this feat). If you're holding one of them, you can load a piece of ammunition into it even if you lack a free hand.

entries.name: Firing in Melee
entries: Being within 5 feet of an enemy doesn't impose {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on your attack rolls with crossbows.

entries.name: Dual Wielding
entries: When you make the extra attack of the {@itemProperty L|XPHB|Light} property, you can add your ability modifier to the damage of the extra attack if that attack is with a crossbow that has the {@itemProperty L|XPHB|Light} property and you aren't already adding that modifier to the damage.

entities.name: Crusher
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Push
entries: Once per turn, when you hit a creature with an attack that deals Bludgeoning damage, you can move it 5 feet to an unoccupied space if the target is no more than one size larger than you.

entries.name: Enhanced Critical
entries: When you score a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB} that deals Bludgeoning damage to a creature, attack rolls against that creature have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} until the start of your next turn.

entities.name: Cult of the Dragon Initiate
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Dragon's Tongue
entries: You know Draconic. If you already know Draconic when you select this feat, you instead learn one language of your choice from the language tables in the Player's Handbook or Chapter 2 of this book.

entries.name: Dragon's Terror
entries: You can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to instill terror in a creature you can see within 30 feet of yourself. The target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw (DC 8 plus your Wisdom modifier and {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}) or have the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition until the end of your next turn. If the target succeeds on the save or when the effect ends for a target, the target is immune to this effect for 24 hours.

entries.name: Inspired by Fear
entries: When you cause a creature to have the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition and you are the source of its fear, you can gain {@variantrule Heroic Inspiration|XPHB} if you lack it. Once you use this benefit, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Defense
entries: While you're wearing Light, Medium, or Heavy armor, you gain a +1 bonus to {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB}.

entities.name: Defensive Duelist
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Parry
entries: If you're holding a Finesse weapon and another creature hits you with a melee attack, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to add your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} to your {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB}, potentially causing the attack to miss you. You gain this bonus to your AC against melee attacks until the start of your next turn.

entities.name: Delicious Pain
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Toughened Flesh
entries: Immediately after you take Bludgeoning, Piercing, or Slashing damage, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to gain {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage until the start of your next turn. Once you use this benefit, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Dragonscarred
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Damage Resistance
entries: When you gain this feat, choose Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Poison. You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to the chosen damage type.

entries.name: Fearsome Power
entries: When you deal damage to a creature as part of the Attack or {@action Magic|XPHB} action on your turn, you can use the Dragon's Terror benefit of the Cult of the Dragon Initiate feat as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} this turn.

entities.name: Druidic Warrior

otherSummary.entry: When Gaining the Level 2 Ranger "Fighting Style" Feature

entries: You learn two {@filter Druid cantrips|spells|level=0|class=druid} of your choice. {@spell Guidance|XPHB} and {@spell Starry Wisp|XPHB} are recommended. The chosen cantrips count as Ranger spells for you, and Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for them. Whenever you gain a Ranger level, you can replace one of these cantrips with another Druid cantrip.

entities.name: Dual Wielder
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Enhanced Dual Wielding
entries: When you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your turn and attack with a weapon that has the {@itemProperty L|XPHB|Light} property, you can make one extra attack as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} later on the same turn with a different weapon, which must be a Melee weapon that lacks the {@itemProperty 2H|XPHB|Two-Handed} property. You don't add your ability modifier to the extra attack's damage unless that modifier is negative.

entries.name: Quick Draw
entries: You can draw or stow two weapons that lack the {@itemProperty 2H|XPHB|Two-Handed} property when you would normally be able to draw or stow only one.

entities.name: Dueling
entries: When you're holding a Melee weapon in one hand and no other weapons, you gain a +2 bonus to damage rolls with that weapon.

entities.name: Durable
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Defy Death
entries: You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on {@variantrule Death Saving Throw|XPHB|Death Saving Throws}.

entries.name: Speedy Recovery
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can expend one of your {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB}, roll the die, and regain a number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the roll.

entities.name: Echoing Soul
entries: You experience echoes from a past or alternate life. You gain the following features.

entries.name: Channeled Prowess
entries: You have proficiency in two skills of your choice.

In addition, choose one skill you have proficiency in. You gain {@variantrule Expertise|XPHB} in that skill. Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can change your choice for this benefit.

entries.name: Inherent Tongues
entries: You know one additional language of your choice, which you choose from the language tables in the Player's Handbook.

entries.name: Intrusive Echoes
entries: Immediately after you make a {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB} and roll a 1 on the {@dice d20}, memories and sensations from your soul's other life threaten to overtake you. Make a Constitution saving throw (DC 13 plus your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}). On a failed save, you have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition until the end of your next turn. While you are {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} in this way, your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is halved.

entities.name: Elemental Adept
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Energy Mastery
entries: Choose one of the following damage types: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder. Spells you cast ignore {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to damage of the chosen type. In addition, when you roll damage for a spell you cast that deals damage of that type, you can treat any 1 on a damage die as a 2.

entries.name: Repeatable
entries: You can take this feat more than once, but you must choose a different damage type each time for Energy Mastery.

entities.name: Emerald Enclave Fledgling
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Speak with Animals
entries: You always have the {@spell Speak with Animals|XPHB} spell prepared and can cast it with any spell slots you have. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for this spell (choose when you select this feat). When you cast this spell as a {@variantrule Ritual|XPHB}, its duration is 8 hours.

entries.name: Tag Team
entries: When you take the {@action Help|XPHB} action, you can switch places with a willing ally within 5 feet of yourself as part of that same action. This movement doesn't provoke {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks}. You can't use this benefit if the ally has the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition.

entities.name: Enclave Magic
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Friend to Animals
entries: You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on ability checks when taking the {@action Influence|XPHB} action with Beasts.

entries.name: Two Hearts, One Mind
entries: You always have the {@spell Beast Sense|XPHB} spell prepared. You can cast it once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it in that way when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. When you cast it without a spell slot using this feature, it doesn't require {@status Concentration|XPHB}. You can also cast the spell using any spell slots you have of the appropriate level. The spell's spellcasting ability is the ability increased by this feat.

entities.name: Fairy Trickster
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Faerie Trod Trotter
entries: When you take the {@action Disengage|XPHB} action on your turn, {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB} doesn't cost you extra movement for the rest of that turn.

entries.name: Flustering Strike
entries: When you hit a creature with an attack roll, you can attempt to fluster the target. The target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw (DC 8 plus the ability modifier of the score increased by this feat and your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}) or have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on saving throws until the end of your next turn.
You can use this benefit a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Fey-Touched
entries: Your exposure to the Feywild's magic grants you the following benefits.

entries.name: Fey Magic
entries: Choose one {@filter level 1 spell from the Divination or Enchantment|spells|level=1|school=E;D} school of magic. You always have that spell and the {@spell Misty Step|XPHB} spell prepared. You can cast each of these spells without expending a spell slot. Once you cast either spell in this way, you can't cast that spell in this way again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also cast these spells using spell slots you have of the appropriate level. The spells' spellcasting ability is the ability increased by this feat.

entities.name: Gathered Whispers
entries: You are haunted by a cacophony of whispering spirits only you can hear. You gain the following features.

entries.name: Spirit Whispers
entries: You learn the {@spell Message|XPHB} spell and can cast it without Material components. Additionally, you always have the {@spell Augury|XPHB} spell prepared. You can cast it without a spell slot or spell components, and you must finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} before you can cast it in this way again. You can also cast the spell using any spell slots you have.
Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for this benefit (choose when you select this feat).

entries.name: Unearthly Scream
entries: When you are hit by an attack roll, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to channel your haunting spirits into a protective, otherworldly scream. You can add your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} to your AC against that attack, potentially causing it to miss. You can use this benefit a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Voices from Beyond
entries: Immediately after you make a {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB} and roll a 1 on the {@dice d20}, the haunting voices rise to a ghastly volume. Make a Wisdom saving throw (DC 13 plus your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}). On a failed save, you have the {@condition Deafened|XPHB} condition until the end of your next turn. While {@condition Deafened|XPHB}, you have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on ability checks and attack rolls.

entities.name: Genie Magic
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Wish Magic
entries: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can cast a {@filter level 1 spell of your choice from the Sorcerer spell list|spells|level=1|class=Sorcerer} that has a casting time of an action. Once you use this benefit, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. The spell's spellcasting ability is the ability increased by this feat.
When you reach level 11, the spell you cast with this feat is cast as though using a level 2 spell slot.
When you reach level 17, the spell is cast as though using a level 3 spell slot.

entities.name: Grappler
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Punch and Grab
entries: When you hit a creature with an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} as part of the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your turn, you can use both the Damage and the Grapple option. You can use this benefit only once per turn.

entries.name: Attack Advantage
entries: You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on attack rolls against a creature {@condition Grappled|XPHB} by you.

entries.name: Fast Wrestler
entries: You don't have to spend extra movement to move a creature {@condition Grappled|XPHB} by you if the creature is your size or smaller.

entities.name: Great Weapon Fighting
entries: When you roll damage for an attack you make with a Melee weapon that you are holding with two hands, you can treat any 1 or 2 on a damage die as a 3. The weapon must have the {@itemProperty 2h|XPHB|Two-Handed} or {@itemProperty V|XPHB|Versatile} property to gain this benefit.

entities.name: Great Weapon Master
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Heavy Weapon Mastery
entries: When you hit a creature with a weapon that has the {@itemProperty H|XPHB|Heavy} property as part of the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your turn, you can cause the weapon to deal extra damage to the target. The extra damage equals your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}.

entries.name: Hew
entries: Immediately after you score a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB} with a Melee weapon or reduce a creature to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} with one, you can make one attack with the same weapon as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}.

entities.name: Greater Aberrant Mark
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Improved Fortitude
entries: When you use the Aberrant Fortitude benefit of your Aberrant Dragonmark feat, you can roll {@dice 1d6} instead of {@dice 1d4}. You also now regain your use of Aberrant Fortitude whenever you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Mark of Inspiration
entries: When you cast a cantrip, you can roll one or two of your unexpended {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB}. You gain a number of {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the number rolled plus your Constitution modifier, and one creature of your choice within 30 feet of you (not including you) takes Force damage equal to the number rolled. Those dice are then expended.
You can use this benefit a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Greater Mark of Detection
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Improved Intuition
entries: When you use the Deductive Intuition benefit of your {@feat Mark of Detection|EFA} feat, you can roll {@dice 1d6} instead of {@dice 1d4}.

entries.name: Shared Detection
entries: When you use the Magical Detection benefit of your {@feat Mark of Detection|EFA} feat to cast {@spell See Invisibility|XPHB} without a spell slot, you can choose one creature you can see within 30 feet of yourself. That creature also gains the benefits of the spell for its duration.

entities.name: Greater Mark of Finding
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Improved Intuition
entries: When you use the Hunter's Intuition benefit of your {@feat Mark of Finding|EFA} feat, you can roll {@dice 1d6} instead of {@dice 1d4}.

entries.name: Improved Finding
entries: When you use the Finder's Magic benefit of your {@feat Mark of Finding|EFA} to cast {@spell Hunter's Mark|XPHB} without a spell slot, the range of the spell is doubled, and you can modify the spell so that the target can't benefit from the {@condition Invisible|XPHB} condition for the duration.

entities.name: Greater Mark of Handling
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Improved Intuition
entries: When you use the Wild Intuition benefit of your {@feat Mark of Handling|EFA} feat, you can roll {@dice 1d6} instead of {@dice 1d4}.

entries.name: Improved Handling
entries: While you are mounted, immediately after you hit a target within 5 feet of your mount with a melee attack roll, your mount can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to move up to its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} or take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action to make one attack only (your choice).

entries.name: Subdue Animal
entries: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you exert command over one Beast or Monstrosity you can see within 30 feet of yourself. The target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw (DC 8 plus your Wisdom modifier and {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}) or have the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition until the start of your next turn. You can use this benefit a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Greater Mark of Healing
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Improved Intuition
entries: When you use the Medical Intuition benefit of your {@feat Mark of Healing|EFA} feat, you can roll {@dice 1d6} instead of {@dice 1d4}.

entries.name: Improved Healing
entries: You can now use the Healing Touch benefit of your {@feat Mark of Healing|EFA} feat to cast {@spell Cure Wounds|XPHB} without using a spell slot a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. Additionally, when you cast {@spell Cure Wounds|XPHB} and roll dice to determine the number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} restored, you can treat any 1 or 2 on a roll as a 3.

entities.name: Greater Mark of Hospitality
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Improved Intuition
entries: When you use the Ever Hospitable benefit of your {@feat Mark of Hospitality|EFA} feat, you can roll {@dice 1d6} instead of {@dice 1d4}.

entries.name: Improved Hospitality
entries: When you cast {@spell Purify Food and Drink|XPHB}, you can modify the spell so that instead of its normal effect, each creature of your choice within 30 feet of you is refreshed. Each affected creature's {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} level is reduced by 1, and the creature gains {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} plus your Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma modifier (choose when you select this feat). Once you modify the spell with this benefit, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Greater Mark of Making
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Improved Intuition
entries: When you use the Artisan's Intuition benefit of your {@feat Mark of Making|EFA} feat, you can roll {@dice 1d6} instead of {@dice 1d4}.

entries.name: Improved Making
entries: When you use the Spellsmith benefit of your {@feat Mark of Making|EFA} feat to cast {@spell Magic Weapon|XPHB} without a spell slot, you cast the spell as its level 3 version.

entities.name: Greater Mark of Passage
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Improved Intuition
entries: When you use the Intuitive Motion benefit of your {@feat Mark of Passage|EFA} feat, you can roll {@dice 1d6} instead of {@dice 1d4}.

entries.name: Improved Passage
entries: When you use the Magical Passage benefit of your {@feat Mark of Passage|EFA} feat to cast {@spell Misty Step|XPHB} without a spell slot, you can also choose up to two willing creatures you can see within 30 feet of yourself before you teleport. Each target can then take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to also teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space it can see.

entities.name: Greater Mark of Scribing
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Improved Intuition
entries: When you use the Gifted Scribe benefit of your {@feat Mark of Scribing|EFA} feat, you can roll {@dice 1d6} instead of {@dice 1d4}.

entries.name: Inspired Scribing
entries: When you cast {@spell Comprehend Languages|XPHB}, you can modify the spell to encompass up to three willing creatures you can see within 30 feet of yourself. Each chosen creature also gains the benefits of the spell for the duration. In addition, for the duration of the spell, you and the chosen creatures can communicate telepathically with each other while within 1 mile of each other. Once you modify the spell with this benefit, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Greater Mark of Sentinel
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Improved Intuition
entries: When you use the Sentinel's Intuition benefit of your {@feat Mark of Sentinel|EFA} feat, you can roll {@dice 1d6} instead of {@dice 1d4}.

entries.name: Improved Sentinel
entries: When you use the Vigilant Guardian benefit of your {@feat Mark of Sentinel|EFA} feat, you can also make one attack with a weapon or an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} as part of that same {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}.

entities.name: Greater Mark of Shadow
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Improved Intuition
entries: When you use the Cunning Intuition benefit of your {@feat Mark of Shadow|EFA} feat, you can roll {@dice 1d6} instead of {@dice 1d4}.

entries.name: Improved Shadow
entries: When you use the Shape Shadows benefit of your {@feat Mark of Shadow|EFA} feat to cast {@spell Invisibility|XPHB} without a spell slot, you cast the spell as its level 3 version.

entities.name: Greater Mark of Storm
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Improved Intuition
entries: When you use the Windwright's Intuition benefit of your {@feat Mark of Storm|EFA} feat, you can roll {@dice 1d6} instead of {@dice 1d4}.

entries.name: Improved Storm
entries: When you use the Storm Magic benefit of your {@feat Mark of Storm|EFA} feat to cast {@spell Gust of Wind|XPHB} without a spell slot, you also gain a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} of 60 feet for the duration of the spell.

entities.name: Greater Mark of Warding
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Improved Intuition
entries: When you use the Warder's Intuition benefit of your {@feat Mark of Warding|EFA} feat, you can roll {@dice 1d6} instead of {@dice 1d4}.

entries.name: Improved Warding
entries: When a creature makes an attack roll against you or a creature you can see within 30 feet of yourself, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to impose {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on that roll. You can use this benefit a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Harper Agent
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Thieves' Cant
entries: You know Thieves' Cant.

entries.name: Instrument Training
entries: You gain proficiency with a {@item Musical Instrument|XPHB} of your choice.

entries.name: Distracting Melody
entries: When you take the {@action Help|XPHB} action to assist an ally's attack roll, the enemy you're distracting can be within 30 feet of you, rather than within 5 feet of you, provided the enemy can see or hear you.

entities.name: Harper Teamwork
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Withering Wordplay
entries: When you take the {@action Help|XPHB} action to assist an ally's attack roll against an enemy, that enemy also has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on the first saving throw it makes before the start of your next turn.

entries.name: Inspiring Willpower
entries: If you succeed on a saving throw to end the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} or {@condition Paralyzed|XPHB} condition on yourself, you can choose one ally you can see within 30 feet of yourself that has the same condition. That condition immediately ends for that ally.

entities.name: Healer
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Battle Medic
entries: If you have a {@item Healer's Kit|XPHB}, you can expend one use of it and tend to a creature within 5 feet of yourself as a {@action Utilize|XPHB} action. That creature can expend one of its {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB}, and you then roll that die. The creature regains a number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the roll plus your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}.

entries.name: Healing Rerolls
entries: Whenever you roll a die to determine the number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} you restore with a spell or with this feat's Battle Medic benefit, you can reroll the die if it rolls a 1, and you must use the new roll.

entities.name: Heavily Armored
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Armor Training
entries: You gain training with Heavy armor.

entities.name: Heavy Armor Master
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Damage Reduction
entries: When you're hit by an attack while you're wearing Heavy armor, any Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage dealt to you by that attack is reduced by an amount equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}.

entities.name: Inspiring Leader
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Bolstering Performance
entries: When you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can give an inspiring performance: a speech, song, or dance. When you do so, choose up to six allies (which can include yourself) within 30 feet of yourself who witness the performance. The chosen creatures each gain {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to your character level plus the modifier of the ability you increased with this feat.

entities.name: Interception
entries: When a creature you can see hits another creature within 5 feet of you with an attack roll, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to reduce the damage dealt to the target by {@dice 1d10} plus your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}. You must be holding a {@item Shield|XPHB} or a Simple or Martial weapon to use this {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}.

entities.name: Keen Mind
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Lore Knowledge
entries: Choose one of the following skills: {@skill Arcana|XPHB}, {@skill History|XPHB}, {@skill Investigation|XPHB}, {@skill Nature|XPHB}, or {@skill Religion|XPHB}. If you lack proficiency in the chosen skill, you gain proficiency in it, and if you already have proficiency in it, you gain {@variantrule Expertise|XPHB} in it.

entries.name: Quick Study
entries: You can take the {@action Study|XPHB} action as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}.

entities.name: Light Bringer
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Sacred Magic
entries: You learn the {@spell Light|XPHB} spell and can cast it without Material components. If you already know that cantrip, you learn a different Cleric cantrip of your choice. The spell's spellcasting ability is the ability increased by this feat.

entries.name: Solar Luminance
entries: When you cast {@spell Light|XPHB}, you can have the light from the spell be sunlight. Once you use this benefit, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Sun's Healing
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} while within sunlight, you can expend one of your {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB}, roll the die, and regain a number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the roll. Once you use this benefit, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Lightly Armored
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Armor Training
entries: You gain training with Light armor and Shields.

entities.name: Living Shadow
entries: The shadow you cast is animate and ever-present—sometimes it even acts according to its own will. You gain the following features.

entries.name: Grasping Shadow
entries: You learn the {@spell Mage Hand|XPHB} spell and can cast it without spell components. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for this spell (choose when you select this feat).

entries.name: Lengthened Strike
entries: When you make a melee attack roll as part of the {@action Attack|XPHB} or {@action Magic|XPHB} action on your turn, you can increase your reach for that attack by 10 feet, as your shadow stretches to aid you. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Ominous Will
entries: Immediately after you make a {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB} and roll a 1 on the {@dice d20}, your shadow attempts to exert its will. Make a Wisdom saving throw (DC 13 plus your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}). On a failed save, you have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition until the start of your next turn, at which point you must roll on the Shadow's Will table to determine what you do during that turn.

entries.caption: Shadow's Will
rows: You don't take an action or a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, and you use all your movement to move. Roll {@dice 1d4} for the direction: 1, north; 2, east; 3, south; or 4, west.

You don't move or take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, and you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action to make one melee attack against a random creature within reach. If none are within reach, you take no action.
You have the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition, and your turn ends.

entities.name: Lordly Resolve
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Standard Bearer
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, choose up to three creatures within 60 feet of yourself that can see you. Each target can immediately take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to right itself and end the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition, provided its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} isn't 0. Additionally, you bolster the targets' resolve, which lasts for 1 minute or until you have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition. While bolstered, a target can't be possessed or gain the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} or {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition; if a target is already possessed, {@condition Charmed|XPHB}, or {@condition Frightened|XPHB}, the target has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on any new saving throw against the relevant effect.
Once you use this benefit, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Lords' Alliance Agent
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Inspiring Strike
entries: Once per turn when you score a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB} against a creature, you can choose an ally within 30 feet of yourself who can see or hear you and who lacks {@variantrule Heroic Inspiration|XPHB}. That ally gains {@variantrule Heroic Inspiration|XPHB}.

entries.name: Reassert Honor
entries: When an enemy you can see deals damage to an ally of yours that is within 5 feet of you, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on your next attack roll against that enemy before the end of your next turn.

entities.name: Love Bites
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Endearing Pain
entries: Immediately after you damage a creature with a Melee weapon or an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to give the target the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} condition until the start of your next turn or until you or your allies damage it. Once you use this benefit, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Lucky
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Luck Points
entries: You have a number of Luck Points equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} and can spend the points on the benefits below. You regain your expended Luck Points when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Advantage
entries: When you roll a {@dice d20} for a {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB}, you can spend 1 Luck Point to give yourself {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the roll.

entries.name: Disadvantage
entries: When a creature rolls a {@dice d20} for an attack roll against you, you can spend 1 Luck Point to impose {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on that roll.

entities.name: Mage Slayer
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Concentration Breaker
entries: When you damage a creature that is {@status Concentration|XPHB|Concentrating}, it has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on the saving throw it makes to maintain {@status Concentration|XPHB}.

entries.name: Guarded Mind
entries: If you fail an Intelligence, a Wisdom, or a Charisma saving throw, you can cause yourself to succeed instead. Once you use this benefit, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Magic Initiate
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Two Cantrips
entries: You learn two cantrips of your choice from the {@filter Cleric|spells|level=0;1|class=Cleric}, {@filter Druid|spells|level=0;1|class=Druid}, or {@filter Wizard|spells|level=0;1|class=Wizard} spell list. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for this feat's spells (choose when you select this feat).

entries.name: Level 1 Spell
entries: Choose a level 1 spell from the same list you selected for this feat's cantrips. You always have that spell prepared. You can cast it once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it in that way when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also cast the spell using any spell slots you have.

entries.name: Spell Change
entries: Whenever you gain a new level, you can replace one of the spells you chose for this feat with a different spell of the same level from the chosen spell list.

entries.name: Repeatable
entries: You can take this feat more than once, but you must choose a different spell list each time.

items.name: Two Cantrips

entries: Choose a level 1 spell from the {@filter Wizard|spells|level=0;1|class=Wizard} spell list. You always have that spell prepared. entities.name: Mark of Detection
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Deductive Intuition
entries: When you make an Intelligence ({@skill Investigation|XPHB}) or Wisdom ({@skill Insight|XPHB}) check, you can roll {@dice 1d4} and add the number rolled to the ability check.

entries.name: Magical Detection
entries: You always have the {@spell Detect Magic|XPHB} and {@spell Detect Poison and Disease|XPHB} spells prepared. You can cast each spell once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it in that way when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also cast these spells using any spell slots you have of the appropriate level. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells (choose when you select this feat).
When you reach character level 3, you also always have the {@spell See Invisibility|XPHB} spell prepared and can cast it the same way.

entries.name: Spells of the Mark
entries: If you have the Spellcasting or Pact Magic class feature, the spells on the Mark of Detection Spells table are added to that feature's spell list.

entities.name: Mark of Finding
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Hunter's Intuition
entries: When you make a Wisdom ({@skill Perception|XPHB} or {@skill Survival|XPHB}) check, you can roll {@dice 1d4} and add the number rolled to the ability check.

entries.name: Finder's Magic
entries: You always have the {@spell Hunter's Mark|XPHB} spell prepared. You can cast it once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it in that way when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also cast it using any spell slots you have of the appropriate level. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for this spell (choose when you select this feat).

entries.caption: Mark of Finding Spells
rows: {@spell Locate Animals or Plants|XPHB}, {@spell Mind Spike|XPHB}

entities.name: Mark of Handling

entries: When you make an Intelligence ({@skill Nature|XPHB}) or Wisdom ({@skill Animal Handling|XPHB}) check, you can roll {@dice 1d4} and add the number rolled to the ability check.

entries.name: Primal Connection
entries: You always have the {@spell Animal Friendship|XPHB} and {@spell Speak with Animals|XPHB} spells prepared. You can cast each spell once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it in that way when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also cast these spells using any spell slots you have. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells (choose when you select this feat).

entries.name: Monstrous Connections
entries: When you reach character level 3, you can target a Monstrosity when you cast {@spell Animal Friendship|XPHB} or {@spell Speak with Animals|XPHB} if the creature's Intelligence score is 3 or lower.

entries.name: Spells of the Mark
entries: If you have the Spellcasting or Pact Magic class feature, the spells on the Mark of Handling Spells table are added to that feature's spell list.

entities.name: Mark of Healing
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Medical Intuition
entries: When you make a Wisdom ({@skill Medicine|XPHB}) check or an ability check using a {@item Herbalism Kit|XPHB}, you can roll {@dice 1d4} and add the number rolled to the ability check.

entries.name: Healing Touch
entries: You always have the {@spell Cure Wounds|XPHB} spell prepared. You can cast it once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it in that way when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. {@spell Aura of Life|XPHB}, {@spell Aura of Purity|XPHB}

entries: When you make a Charisma ({@skill Persuasion|XPHB}) check or an ability check using {@item Brewer's Supplies|XPHB} or {@item Cook's Utensils|XPHB}, you can roll {@dice 1d4} and add the number rolled to the ability check.

entries.name: Innkeeper's Magic

You can cast each spell once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it in that way when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also cast these spells using any spell slots you have of the appropriate level. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells (choose when you select this feat).

{@spell Aura of Purity|XPHB}, {@spell Mordenkainen's Private Sanctum|XPHB}

entries: When you make an Intelligence ({@skill Arcana|XPHB}) check or an ability check using {@item Artisan's Tools|XPHB}, you can roll {@dice 1d4} and add the number rolled to the ability check.

entries.name: Spellsmith

You can cast it once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it in that way when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also cast it using any spell slots you have. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells (choose when you select this feat).

entries.name: Spells of the Mark
entries: If you have the Spellcasting or Pact Magic class feature, the spells on the Mark of Making Spells table are added to that feature's spell list.

entries.name: Courier's Speed
entries: Your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} increases by 5 feet.

entries.name: Intuitive Motion
entries: When you make a Strength ({@skill Athletics|XPHB}) or Dexterity ({@skill Acrobatics|XPHB}) check, you can roll {@dice 1d4} and add the number rolled to the ability check.

entries.name: Magical Passage
entries: You always have the {@spell Misty Step|XPHB} spell prepared. You can cast it once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it in that way when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also cast it using any spell slots you have. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for this spell (choose when you select this feat).

entries.name: Spells of the Mark
entries: If you have the Spellcasting or Pact Magic class feature, the spells on the Mark of Passage Spells table are added to that feature's spell list.

entries.name: Scribe's Insight

You can also cast it using any spell slots you have of the appropriate level. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for this spell (choose when you select this feat).

entries.name: Guardian's Shield
entries: You always have the {@spell Shield|XPHB} spell prepared. You can cast it once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it in that way when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also cast it using any spell slots you have. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for this spell (choose when you select this feat).

entries.name: Vigilant Guardian
entries: When a creature you can see within 5 feet of you is hit by an attack roll, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to swap places with that creature, and you are hit by the attack instead. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} and regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Spells of the Mark
entries: If you have the Spellcasting or Pact Magic class feature, the spells on the Mark of Sentinel Spells table are added to that feature's spell list.

entries.name: Cunning Intuition
entries: When you make a Dexterity ({@skill Stealth|XPHB}) or Charisma ({@skill Performance|XPHB}) check, you can roll {@dice 1d4} and add the number rolled to the ability check.

entries.name: Shape Shadows
entries: You know the {@spell Minor Illusion|XPHB} cantrip. You also always have the {@spell Invisibility|XPHB} spell prepared. You can cast it once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it in that way when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also cast it using any spell slots you have of the appropriate level. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells (choose when you select this feat).

entries: When you make a Dexterity ({@skill Acrobatics|XPHB}) check or an ability check using {@item Navigator's Tools|XPHB}, you can roll {@dice 1d4} and add the number rolled to the ability check.

entries.name: Storm's Boon
entries: You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Lightning damage.

entries.name: Storm Magic
entries: You know the {@spell Thunderclap|XPHB} cantrip. When you reach character level 3, you also always have the {@spell Gust of Wind|XPHB} spell prepared. You can cast it once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it in that way when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also cast these spells using any spell slots you have of the appropriate level. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells (choose when you select this feat).

entries: If you have the Spellcasting or Pact Magic class feature, the spells on the Mark of Warding Spells table are added to that feature's spell list.
