entities.name: Martial Weapon Training
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Weapon Proficiency
entries: You gain proficiency with {@filter Martial weapons|items|type=martial weapon}.

entities.name: Medium Armor Master
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Dexterous Wearer
entries: While you're wearing Medium armor, you can add 3, rather than 2 to your AC if you have a Dexterity score of 16 or higher.

entities.name: Mist Walker
entries: You know how to slip through the Mists' grasp, but this freedom comes at a price: If you remain in one area for too long, the Mists find you and drain your life force. You gain the following features.

entries.name: Domain Traveler
entries: When you enter the Mists intent on reaching a specific domain, you are treated as if you possess a Mist talisman keyed to that domain. To use this feature, you must know the name of the domain you have chosen as your destination, but you don't need to have previously visited that land. This trait doesn't allow you to bypass domain borders closed by a Darklord's will.

entries.name: Mist Walk
entries: When you take damage or fail a saving throw to avoid or end the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} or {@condition Restrained|XPHB} condition, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} and teleport up to 15 feet to an unoccupied space you can see. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Poisoned Roots
entries: When you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, the world around you in a 10-mile radius becomes a siphon that leeches away at your vitality. Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} in that area, make a Constitution saving throw (DC 13 plus your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}). On a failed save, you get no benefits from finishing that rest.

entities.name: Moderately Armored
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Armor Training
entries: You gain training with Medium armor.

entities.name: Mounted Combatant
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Mounted Strike
entries: While {@book mounted|XPHB|1|Mounted Combat}, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on attack rolls against any unmounted creature within 5 feet of your mount that is at least one size smaller than the mount.

entries.name: Leap Aside
entries: If your mount is subjected to an effect that allows it to make a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage, it instead takes no damage if it succeeds on the saving throw and only half damage if it fails. For your mount to gain this benefit, you must be riding it, and neither of you can have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition.

entries.name: Veer
entries: While {@book mounted|XPHB|1|Mounted Combat}, you can force an attack that hits your mount to hit you instead if you don't have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition.

entities.name: Musician
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Instrument Training
entries: You gain proficiency with three Musical Instruments of your choice.

entries.name: Encouraging Song
entries: As you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can play a song on a {@item Musical Instrument|XPHB} with which you have proficiency and give {@variantrule Heroic Inspiration|XPHB} to allies who hear the song. The number of allies you can affect in this way equals your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}.

entries.name: Mythal Ward
entries: If a spell attack hits you or you fail a saving throw against a spell, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to roll on the Mythal-Touched {@action Magic|XPHB} table to create a magical effect. If an effect requires a saving throw, the DC equals 8 plus the modifier of the ability increased by this feat and your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}.
You can use this benefit a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.caption: Mythal-Touched Magic
rows: You and each creature within 15 feet of you make a Dexterity saving throw, taking Force damage equal to {@dice 1d8} times the level of the triggering spell on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.
You and the triggering spell's caster form a telepathic link for 1 hour.
Gravity is reversed in a 15-foot-radius, 60-foot-tall {@variantrule Cylinder [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cylinder} centered on you for 1 minute, per the {@spell Reverse Gravity|XPHB} spell.
You and the triggering spell's caster each make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the creature has the {@condition Stunned|XPHB} condition until the end of its next turn.
You gain a +2 bonus to AC for 1 minute, potentially turning the triggering spell into a miss if it was a spell attack.

Any flammable, nonmagical object within 10 feet of the triggering spell's caster that isn't being worn or carried by another creature bursts into flame, takes {@damage 1d4} Fire damage, and is burning.

The triggering spell dissipates with no effect, and the action, {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, or {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} used to cast it is wasted. If that spell was cast with a spell slot, the slot isn't expended.

entities.name: Observant
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Keen Observer
entries: Choose one of the following skills: {@skill Insight|XPHB}, {@skill Investigation|XPHB}, or {@skill Perception|XPHB}. If you lack proficiency with the chosen skill, you gain proficiency in it, and if you already have proficiency in it, you gain {@variantrule Expertise|XPHB} in it.

entries.name: Quick Search
entries: You can take the {@action Search|XPHB} action as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}.

entities.name: Order's Resilience
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Resurge
entries: When you have the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition, you can right yourself with only 5 feet of movement.

entries.name: Stronger Together
entries: If you are within 5 feet of an ally that doesn't have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition, you and that ally have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Strength saving throws. You can't use this benefit while you have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition.

entities.name: Piercer
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Puncture
entries: Once per turn, when you hit a creature with an attack that deals Piercing damage, you can reroll one of the attack's damage dice, and you must use the new roll.

entries.name: Enhanced Critical
entries: When you score a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB} that deals Piercing damage to a creature, you can roll one additional damage die when determining the extra Piercing damage the target takes.

entities.name: Poisoner
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Potent Poison
entries: When you make a damage roll that deals Poison damage, it ignores {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Poison damage.

entries.name: Brew Poison
entries: You gain proficiency with the {@item Poisoner's Kit|XPHB}. With 1 hour of work using such a kit and expending 50 GP worth of materials, you can create a number of poison doses equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}. As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can apply a poison dose to a weapon or piece of ammunition. Once applied, the poison retains its potency for 1 minute or until you deal damage with the poisoned item, whichever is shorter. When a creature takes damage from the poisoned item, that creature must succeed on a Constitution saving throw ({@dc 8} plus the modifier of the ability increased by this feat and your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}) or take {@damage 2d8} Poison damage and have the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition until the end of your next turn.

entities.name: Polearm Master
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Pole Strike
entries: Immediately after you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action and attack with a {@item Quarterstaff|XPHB}, a {@item Spear|XPHB}, or a {@filter weapon that has the Heavy and Reach properties|items|type=melee weapon;ranged weapon|property=heavy;reach=sand}, you can use a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to make a melee attack with the opposite end of the weapon. The weapon deals Bludgeoning damage, and the weapon's damage die for this attack is a {@dice d4}.

entries.name: Reactive Strike
entries: While you're holding a {@item Quarterstaff|XPHB}, a {@item Spear|XPHB}, or a {@filter weapon that has the Heavy and Reach properties|items|type=melee weapon;ranged weapon|property=heavy;reach=sand}, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to make one melee attack against a creature that enters the reach you have with that weapon.

entities.name: Potent Dragonmark

choose.entry: Increase the spellcasting ability score used by your Dragonmark feat by 1, to a maximum of 20.

entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Dragonmark Preparation
entries: You always have the spells on your Spells of the Mark list (if any) prepared.

entries.name: Dragonmark Spellcasting
entries: You have one extra spell slot to cast the spells granted by your Dragonmark feat. The spell slot's level is half your level (round up), to a maximum of level 5. You regain the expended slot when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can use this spell slot to cast only a spell you have prepared because of your Dragonmark feat or the Dragonmark Preparation benefit of this feat.

entities.name: Protection
entries: When a creature you can see attacks a target other than you that is within 5 feet of you, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to interpose your {@item Shield|XPHB} if you're holding one. You impose {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on the triggering attack roll and all other attack rolls against the target until the start of your next turn if you remain within 5 feet of the target.

entries.name: Encourage Ally
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you bolster one ally you can see within 30 feet. The ally gains {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to {@dice 2d6} plus the modifier of the ability score increased by this feat. You can take this {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, and you regain all uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Last Stand
entries: You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on attack rolls while {@status Bloodied|XPHB}.

entities.name: Purple Dragon Rook
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Entreat
entries: You gain proficiency in one of the following skills: {@skill Insight|XPHB}, {@skill Performance|XPHB}, or {@skill Persuasion|XPHB}.

entries.name: Rallying Cry
entries: When you roll {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} and don't have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition, you can choose a number of creatures equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} that you can see within 30 feet of yourself. Those creatures gain {@variantrule Heroic Inspiration|XPHB}.
Once you use this benefit, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Putrefy
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Necrosis
entries: When you make a damage roll that deals Necrotic damage, you can cause one creature taking that damage to have the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition until the start of your next turn. Once you use this benefit, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Rebuke
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Radiant Strike
entries: When you make a damage roll that deals Radiant damage, you can cause one Huge or smaller creature taking the damage to have the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition. Once you use this benefit, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Resilient

choose.entry: Choose one ability in which you lack saving throw proficiency. Increase the chosen ability score by 1, to a maximum of 20.

entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Saving Throw Proficiency
entries: You gain saving throw proficiency with the chosen ability.

entities.name: Ritual Caster
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Ritual Spells
entries: Choose a number of level 1 spells equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} that have the {@filter Ritual tag|spells|level=1|components & miscellaneous=ritual}. You always have those spells prepared, and you can cast them with any spell slots you have. The spells' spellcasting ability is the ability increased by this feat. Whenever your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} increases thereafter, you can add an additional level 1 spell with the {@variantrule Ritual|XPHB} tag to the spells always prepared with this feature.

entries.name: Quick Ritual
entries: With this benefit, you can cast a {@variantrule Ritual|XPHB} spell that you have prepared using its regular casting time rather than the extended time for a {@variantrule Ritual|XPHB}. Doing so doesn't require a spell slot. Once you cast the spell in this way, you can't use this benefit again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Savage Attacker
entries: You've trained to deal particularly damaging strikes. Once per turn when you hit a target with a weapon, you can roll the weapon's damage dice twice and use either roll against the target.

entities.name: Second Skin
entries: There is another side of you that most people never see: a beast, a terrifying avenger, or a walking nightmare. You gain the following features.

entries.name: Alternate Form
entries: You always have the {@spell Alter Self|XPHB} spell prepared. You can cast it without a spell slot or spell components, and you must finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} before you can cast it in this way again. You can also cast it using spell slots you have of the appropriate level. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for this spell (choose when you select this feat).
When you cast {@spell Alter Self|XPHB} without a spell slot using this feature, it doesn't require {@status Concentration|XPHB}.

entries.name: Involuntary Change
entries: Certain circumstances can involuntarily trigger your transformation. When you select this feat, roll on the Change Catalyst table to determine what triggers your change.

After you experience the catalyst, at the start of your next turn, make a Charisma saving throw (DC 13 plus your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}). On a failed save, you immediately use Alternate Form to cast {@spell Alter Self|XPHB} without a spell slot. If you've already expended the use of that feature, you instead have the {@condition Stunned|XPHB} condition until the start of your next turn.

entries.caption: Change Catalyst
rows: Seeing a particular phase of the moon
Smelling the scent of a certain type of flower
Touching pure silver with your bare skin
Seeing someone who resembles a specific individual

entities.name: Sentinel
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Guardian
entries: Immediately after a creature within 5 feet of you takes the {@action Disengage|XPHB} action or hits a target other than you with an attack, you can make an {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB} against that creature.

entries.name: Halt
entries: When you hit a creature with an {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB}, the creature's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} becomes 0 for the rest of the current turn.

entities.name: Shadow-Touched
entries: Your exposure to the Shadowfell's magic grants you the following benefits.

entries.name: Shadow Magic
entries: Choose one {@filter level 1 spell from the Illusion or Necromancy|spells|level=1|school=I;N} school of magic. You always have that spell and the {@spell Invisibility|XPHB} spell prepared. You can cast each of these spells without expending a spell slot. Once you cast either spell in this way, you can't cast that spell in this way again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also cast these spells using spell slots you have of the appropriate level. The spells' spellcasting ability is the ability increased by this feat.

entities.name: Shadowmoor Hexer
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Hex
entries: You always have the {@spell Hex|XPHB} spell prepared. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for this spell (choose when you select this feat). You can cast it once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it in that way when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also cast the spell using any spell slots you have.

entries.name: Curse Magic
entries: When a creature that you've cursed with {@spell Hex|XPHB} hits you with an attack roll, the creature takes Psychic damage equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}. A creature takes this damage only once per turn.

entities.name: Sharp Eye
entries: When you take the {@action Search|XPHB} or {@action Study|XPHB} action, you can give yourself {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on any ability check made as part of that action. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. If the check fails, the use of this feature isn't expended.

entities.name: Sharpshooter
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Bypass Cover
entries: Your ranged attacks with weapons ignore {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Half Cover} and {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Three-Quarters Cover}.

entries.name: Firing in Melee
entries: Being within 5 feet of an enemy doesn't impose {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on your attack rolls with Ranged weapons.

entries.name: Long Shots
entries: Attacking at long range doesn't impose {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on your attack rolls with Ranged weapons.

entities.name: Shield Master
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Shield Bash
entries: If you attack a creature within 5 feet of you as part of the {@action Attack|XPHB} action and hit with a Melee weapon, you can immediately bash the target with your {@item Shield|XPHB} if it's equipped, forcing the target to make a Strength saving throw ({@dc 8} plus your Strength modifier and {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}). On a failed save, you either push the target 5 feet from you or cause it to have the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition (your choice). You can use this benefit only once on each of your turns.

entries.name: Interpose Shield
entries: If you're subjected to an effect that allows you to make a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to take no damage if you succeed on the saving throw and are holding a {@item Shield|XPHB}.

entities.name: Skill Expert
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Skill Proficiency
entries: You gain proficiency in one skill of your choice.

entries.name: Expertise
entries: Choose one skill in which you have proficiency but lack {@variantrule Expertise|XPHB}. You gain {@variantrule Expertise|XPHB} with that skill.

entities.name: Skilled
entries: You gain proficiency in any combination of three {@table Skill List; Skills|XPHB|skills} or {@filter tools|items|type=tool;artisan's tools;instrument;gaming set} of your choice.

entries.name: Repeatable
entries: You can take this feat more than once.

entities.name: Skulker
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Blindsight
entries: You have {@sense Blindsight|XPHB} with a range of 10 feet.

entries.name: Fog of War
entries: You exploit the distractions of battle, gaining {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on any Dexterity ({@skill Stealth|XPHB}) check you make as part of the {@action Hide|XPHB} action during combat.

entries.name: Sniper
entries: If you make an attack roll while hidden and the roll misses, making the attack roll doesn't reveal your location.

entities.name: Slasher
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Hamstring
entries: Once per turn when you hit a creature with an attack that deals Slashing damage, you can reduce the {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} of that creature by 10 feet until the start of your next turn.

entries.name: Enhanced Critical
entries: When you score a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB} that deals Slashing damage to a creature, it has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on attack rolls until the start of your next turn.

entities.name: Speedy
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Speed Increase
entries: Your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} increases by 10 feet.

entries.name: Dash over Difficult Terrain
entries: When you take the {@action Dash|XPHB} action on your turn, {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB} doesn't cost you extra movement for the rest of that turn.

entries.name: Agile Movement
entries: {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks} have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} against you.

entities.name: Spell Sniper
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Bypass Cover
entries: Your attack rolls for spells ignore {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Half Cover} and {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Three-Quarters Cover}.

entries.name: Casting in Melee
entries: Being within 5 feet of an enemy doesn't impose {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on your attack rolls with spells.

entries.name: Increased Range
entries: When you cast a spell that has a range of at least 10 feet and requires you to make an attack roll, you can increase the spell's range by 60 feet.

entities.name: Spellfire Adept
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Fueled Spellfire
entries: Once per turn, when a spell you cast deals Radiant damage, you can expend up to two {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB}, roll them, and add the total rolled to one damage roll of the spell.

entries.name: Searing Spellfire
entries: When you make a damage roll that deals Radiant damage, it ignores {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Radiant damage.

entities.name: Spellfire Spark
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Magic Absorption
entries: Once per turn, when you take damage from a spell or magical effect, you reduce the total damage taken by {@dice 1d4}. You can't use this benefit if you have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition.

entries.name: Spellfire Flame
entries: You learn the {@spell Sacred Flame|XPHB} cantrip. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for this spell (choose when you select this feat). You can also cast this cantrip as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Street Justice
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Headlock
entries: Your allies have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on attack rolls against a creature {@condition Grappled|XPHB} by you.

entries.name: Sturdy Knot
entries: When you use {@item Chain|XPHB}, {@item Manacles|XPHB}, or {@item Rope|XPHB} to bind a creature, add your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} to the DC to escape or burst the {@item Chain|XPHB}, {@item Manacles|XPHB}, or {@item Rope|XPHB}.

entries.name: Tough Talk
entries: A creature's {@variantrule Hostile [Attitude]|XPHB|Hostile} attitude doesn't impose {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on your Charisma ({@skill Intimidation|XPHB}) checks to influence that creature.

entities.name: Survivor
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Hypervigilance
entries: Whenever you roll {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB}, you can reroll the {@dice d20} if the number rolled is 9 or lower. You must use the new roll.

entries.name: Steel Yourself
entries: When you fail a saving throw to avoid or end the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} or {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to add a bonus to the roll, potentially causing it to succeed. The bonus is equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}.
Once you take this {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Symbiotic Being
entries: A second being resides within your body, offering knowledge and assistance while furthering its own agenda. You gain the following features.

entries.name: Entwined Existence
entries: The symbiote can't be targeted. If you die, so does your symbiote. If you are returned to life, your symbiote also revives.

entries.name: Second Mind
entries: You gain proficiency in one of the following skills: {@skill Arcana|XPHB}, {@skill Deception|XPHB}, {@skill History|XPHB}, {@skill Intimidation|XPHB}, {@skill Insight|XPHB}, {@skill Investigation|XPHB}, {@skill Nature|XPHB}, {@skill Religion|XPHB}, {@skill Perception|XPHB}, or {@skill Persuasion|XPHB}. You also know one additional language of your choice, chosen from the language tables in the Player's Handbook.

entries.name: Sustained Symbiosis
entries: When you fail a saving throw, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} and expend one of your Hit Dice. Roll the die and add the number rolled to the saving throw, potentially turning the failure into a success.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Symbiotic Agenda
entries: Immediately after you make a {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB} and roll a 1 on the {@dice d20}, your symbiote attempts to assert control. Make a Charisma saving throw (DC 13 plus your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}). On a failed save, you have the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} condition for {@dice 1d12} hours. While {@condition Charmed|XPHB}, you must try to follow the symbiote's commands and further its goals, as determined by the DM. Whenever you take damage, you can repeat this save, ending the effect on a success.
At the DM's discretion, you might make this saving throw whenever you act contrary to the symbiote's agenda.

entities.name: Tavern Brawler
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Enhanced Unarmed Strike
entries: When you hit with your {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} and deal damage, you can deal Bludgeoning damage equal to {@damage 1d4} plus your Strength modifier instead of the normal damage of an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}.

entries.name: Damage Rerolls
entries: Whenever you roll a damage die for your {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, you can reroll the die if it rolls a 1, and you must use the new roll.

entries.name: Improvised Weaponry
entries: You have proficiency with improvised weapons.

entries.name: Push
entries: When you hit a creature with an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} as part of the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your turn, you can deal damage to the target and also push it 5 feet away from you. You can use this benefit only once per turn.

entities.name: Telekinetic
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Minor Telekinesis
entries: You learn the {@spell Mage Hand|XPHB} spell. You can cast it without Verbal or Somatic components, you can make the spectral hand {@condition Invisible|XPHB}, and its range and the distance it can be away from you both increase by 30 feet when you cast it. The spell's spellcasting ability is the ability increased by this feat.

entries.name: Telekinetic Shove
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can telekinetically shove one creature you can see within 30 feet of yourself. When you do so, the target must succeed on a Strength saving throw ({@dc 8} plus the ability modifier of the score increased by this feat and your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}) or be moved 5 feet toward or away from you.

entities.name: Telepathic
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Telepathic Utterance
entries: You can speak telepathically to any creature you can see within 60 feet of yourself. Your telepathic utterances are in a language you know, and the creature understands you only if it knows that language. Your communication doesn't give the creature the ability to respond to you telepathically.

entries.name: Detect Thoughts
entries: You always have the {@spell Detect Thoughts|XPHB} spell prepared. You can cast it without a spell slot or spell components, and you must finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} before you can cast it in this way again. You can also cast it using spell slots you have of the appropriate level. Your spellcasting ability for the spell is the ability increased by this feat.

entities.name: Thrown Weapon Fighting
entries: When you hit with a ranged attack roll using a weapon that has the {@itemProperty T|XPHB|Thrown} property, you gain a +2 bonus to the damage roll.

entities.name: Tireless Reveler
entries: When an ally you can see within 60 feet of yourself expends {@variantrule Heroic Inspiration|XPHB}, you can gain {@variantrule Heroic Inspiration|XPHB} if you lack it. You can use this benefit a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Touch of Death
entries: Deathly power resides within you, bursting out at the slightest provocation. You gain the following features.

entries.name: Death Touch
entries: You learn the {@spell Chill Touch|XPHB} spell and can cast it without spell components. Necrotic damage you deal with this spell ignores {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB}. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for this spell (choose when you select this feat).

entries.name: Pull of the Grave
entries: You have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on {@variantrule Death Saving Throw|XPHB|Death Saving Throws}.

entities.name: Tough
entries: Your {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} maximum increases by an amount equal to twice your character level when you gain this feat. Whenever you gain a character level thereafter, your {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} maximum increases by an additional 2 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}.

entities.name: Treacherous Allure
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Enchanting Presence
entries: You always have the {@spell Charm Person|XPHB} spell prepared. You can cast it without a spell slot, and you must finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} before you can cast it in this way again. You can also cast it using spell slots you have of the appropriate level. Your spellcasting ability for the spell is the ability increased by this feat.

entries.name: Inevitable Betrayal
entries: You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on attack rolls against creatures with the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} condition.

entities.name: Two-Weapon Fighting
entries: When you make an extra attack as a result of using a weapon that has the {@itemProperty L|XPHB|Light} property, you can add your ability modifier to the damage of that attack if you aren't already adding it to the damage.

entities.name: Tyro of the Gauntlet
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Stand as One
entries: When an ally within 5 feet of you is subjected to an effect that would push or pull it, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to prevent that ally from being pushed or pulled. To receive this benefit, the ally can't have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition.

entries.name: Vigilant
entries: When you take the {@action Ready|XPHB} action, the next attack roll made against you has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} before the start of your next turn.

entities.name: Unarmed Fighting
entries: When you hit with your {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} and deal damage, you can deal Bludgeoning damage equal to {@damage 1d6} plus your Strength modifier instead of the normal damage of an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}. If you aren't holding any weapons or a {@item Shield|XPHB} when you make the attack roll, the {@dice d6} becomes a {@dice d8}.
At the start of each of your turns, you can deal {@damage 1d4} Bludgeoning damage to one creature {@condition Grappled|XPHB} by you.

entities.name: Vampire Hunter
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Adroit Escape
entries: You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on checks to escape from nonmagical restraints or the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition.

entries.name: Vitality Ward
entries: When you take Necrotic damage, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to mitigate the damage. Roll a number of d6s equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, and add them together. Reduce the Necrotic damage you take by this total. Once you use this benefit, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Vampire Touched
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Vampire Magic
entries: Choose one level 1 spell from the Enchantment or Illusion school of magic. You always have that spell and the {@spell Spider Climb|XPHB} spell prepared. You can cast each of these spells without expending a spell slot, but when you cast {@spell Spider Climb|XPHB} this way, you must target yourself, and you must finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} before you can cast each spell in this way again. You can also cast either spell using spell slots you have of the appropriate level. Your spellcasting ability for the spells is the ability increased by this feat.

entities.name: Vampire's Plaything
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Decanting
entries: When you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can create one {@item Potion of Healing|XDMG} or an {@item Antitoxin|XPHB}, as long as you have an empty {@item vial|XPHB} or {@item flask|XPHB}. These liquids evaporate when you finish another {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Timely Retreat
entries: You can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to take the {@action Dash|XPHB} action or the {@action Disengage|XPHB} action. You can use this benefit a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, and you recover all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Vampiric Connection
entries: The DM determines the fate of your former vampire master. While you and your former vampire master are on the same plane of existence, the vampire can communicate with you telepathically, and you can choose to allow the vampire to perceive through your senses.

entities.name: War Caster
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Concentration
entries: You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Constitution saving throws that you make to maintain {@status Concentration|XPHB}.

entries.name: Reactive Spell
entries: When a creature provokes an {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB} from you by leaving your reach, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to cast a spell at the creature rather than making an {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB}. The spell must have a casting time of one action and must target only that creature.

entries.name: Somatic Components
entries: You can perform the Somatic components of spells even when you have weapons or a {@item Shield|XPHB} in one or both hands.

entities.name: Watchers
entries: Something unnatural is always watching you, taking the form of scurrying vermin and other eerie creatures. You gain the following features.

entries.name: Borrowed Eyes
entries: You always have the {@spell Beast Sense|XPHB} and {@spell Speak with Animals|XPHB} spells prepared. You can cast each spell without a spell slot, and you must finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} before you can cast it in this way again. You can also cast these spells using spell slots you have of the appropriate level.

entries.name: Heightened Suspicion
entries: Whenever you take the {@action Search|XPHB} action, you can roll {@dice 1d4} and add the number rolled to any ability check made as part of that action.

entries.name: Incessant Watchers
entries: You have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on saving throws made against the {@spell Scrying|XPHB} spell.

In addition, immediately after you make a {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB} and roll a 1 on the {@dice d20}, paranoia threatens to overwhelm you. Make a Wisdom saving throw (DC 13 plus your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}). On a failed save, you have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB|D20 Tests} for 1 minute. You can repeat the save at the end of each of your turns, ending the effect early on a success.

entities.name: Weapon Master
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Mastery Property
entries: Your training with weapons allows you to use the mastery property of one kind of Simple or Martial weapon of your choice, provided you have proficiency with it. Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can change the kind of weapon to another eligible kind.

entities.name: Zhentarim Ruffian
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Exploit Opening
entries: When you roll damage for an {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB}, you can roll the damage dice twice and use either roll against the target.

entries.name: Family First
entries: If you have {@variantrule Heroic Inspiration|XPHB} when you roll {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB}, you can expend it to give yourself and your allies {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on that {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} roll.

entities.name: Zhentarim Tactics
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Retaliate
entries: Immediately after a creature within 5 feet of you hits you with a melee attack, you can make an {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB} against that creature.

entries.name: Versatile Merc
entries: When you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, choose a skill in which you have proficiency. You have {@variantrule Expertise|XPHB} in that skill until you finish your next {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Advanced Weapon Proficiency (FS)
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Weapon Proficiency
entries: You have proficiency with {@filter Advanced weapons|items|type=advanced weapon}, and your training allows you to use the mastery properties of those weapons.

entities.name: Advanced Weapon Proficiency (G)
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Weapon Proficiency
entries: You have proficiency with {@filter Advanced weapons|items|type=advanced weapon}, and your training allows you to use the mastery properties of those weapons.

entities.name: Blackpowder Pistol Expert
entries: You have become practiced with the use of {@item Blackpowder Pistol|GrimHollowPG24|Blackpowder Pistols}. You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Deadeye
entries: Attacking at long range doesn't impose {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on your attack rolls with a {@item Blackpowder Pistol|GrimHollowPG24}.

entries.name: Quick Load
entries: You ignore {@itemProperty LD|XPHB|Loading} of the {@item Blackpowder Pistol|GrimHollowPG24}.

entries.name: Trick Shot
entries: Immediately after a creature within 5 feet of you moves, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to make a ranged attack with a {@item Blackpowder Pistol|GrimHollowPG24} against that creature.

entities.name: Blood Hound
entries: Your senses are heightened beyond those of most folk. Whether these senses were strengthened through training or the loss of other senses, or they simply matured as you did, you are a master of finding those whom you seek. You  gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Motion Sensor
entries: Whenever a creature that is Small or larger moves within 10 feet of you while you do not have the {@condition Unconscious|XPHB} condition, you immediately become aware of its presence.

entries.name: No Hiding
entries: You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Wisdom ({@skill Perception|XPHB}) checks that rely on sound or smell.

entities.name: Boon of Magic Resistance
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Heroic Resistance
entries: If you fail a saving throw, you can cause yourself to succeed instead. Once you use this benefit, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Boon of Perfect Flight
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Flying
entries: You have a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} of 40 feet and can hover.

entries.name: Graceful Fall
entries: If you fall more than 5 feet, your rate of descent slows to 60 feet per round until you land.

entities.name: Boon of Shadowsteel Mastery
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Strange Bedfellows
entries: You are no longer affected by the {@optfeature Friendless|GrimHollowPG24|Friendless Transformation Flaw}.

entities.name: Boon of the Archlich
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Vessel of Vitality
entries: Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, if your {@optfeature Soul Vessel|GrimHollowPG24} is not charged, it gains a soul and becomes charged.

entities.name: Boon of the Ascended Vampire
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Inured to Sunlight
entries: You are no longer affected by sunlight.

entities.name: Boon of the Earthly Tether
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Persistent Haunter
entries: You are no longer affected by the {@optfeature Fraying Reality|GrimHollowPG24|Fraying Reality Transformation Flaw}.

entries.name: Divergent Growth
entries: Choose any {@filter Specter Transformation Boon|optionalfeatures|source=grimhollowpg24|feature type=spec:tb|miscellaneous=} that you don't already have but meet the prerequisites for. You gain that Boon.

entities.name: Boon of the Elder Fey
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Fey Bulwark
entries: You are no longer affected by the {@optfeature Weakened Constitution|GrimHollowPG24|Weakened Constitution Transformation Flaw}.

entries.name: Divergent Growth
entries: Choose any {@filter Fey Transformation Boon|optionalfeatures|source=grimhollowpg24|feature type=f:tb|miscellaneous=} that you don't already have but meet the prerequisites for. You gain that Boon.

entries.name: Free Agent
entries: You are no longer affected by the {@optfeature Pull of the Netherworld|GrimHollowPG24|Pull of the Netherworld Transformation Flaw}.

entries.name: Divergent Growth
entries: Choose any {@filter Fiend Transformation Boon|optionalfeatures|source=grimhollowpg24|feature type=fi:tb|miscellaneous=} that you don't already have but meet the prerequisites for. You gain that Boon.

entities.name: Boon of the Elder Horror
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Stabilizing Form
entries: When you roll a 25 or lower on the {@table Unstable Form|GrimHollowPG24|Unstable Form table}, you can choose to roll again. You must use the new result. Once you use this benefit, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Divergent Growth
entries: Choose any {@filter Aberrant Horror Transformation Boon|optionalfeatures|source=grimhollowpg24|feature type=ah:tb|miscellaneous=} that you don't already have but meet the prerequisites for. You gain that Boon.

entities.name: Boon of the Elemental Temperance
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Controlled Chaos
entries: You are no longer affected by the {@optfeature Primordial Chaos|GrimHollowPG24|Primordial Chaos Transformation Flaw}.

entities.name: Boon of the High Seraph
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Absolution
entries: You are no longer affected by the {@optfeature Seraph Corruption|GrimHollowPG24|Seraph Corruption Transformation Flaw}.

entries.name: Divergent Growth
entries: Choose any {@filter Seraph Transformation Boon|optionalfeatures|source=grimhollowpg24|feature type=ser:tb|miscellaneous=} that you don't already have but meet the prerequisites for. You gain that Boon.

entities.name: Boon of the Wilds
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Apex Predator
entries: When you enter your Hybrid Form, you gain 25 {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB}. At the end of each of your turns while in Hybrid Form, if you have no {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB}, you gain 10 {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB}. While you are in your Hybrid Form and have no {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB}, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on attack rolls.

entities.name: Close Combat Artillerist
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Firing in Melee
entries: Being within 5 feet of an enemy doesn't impose {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on your attack rolls with Ranged weapons.

entries.name: Point Blank
entries: When you hit a creature that is within 5 feet of you with a ranged attack, you gain a +2 bonus to the damage roll.

entities.name: Convincing Inquisitor
entries: You know how to spot a liar, and you can get people to see your way, whether through charm, reasonable arguments, or force of will. You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Charming Presence
entries: When you take the {@action Influence|XPHB} action, you don't have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on checks to influence {@variantrule Hostile [Attitude]|XPHB|Hostile} creatures, and you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on checks to influence {@variantrule Indifferent [Attitude]|XPHB|Indifferent} creatures.

entries.name: Multiple Paths
entries: You can use any ability modifier when you take the {@action Influence|XPHB} action to make an {@skill Intimidation|XPHB} or  {@skill Persuasion|XPHB} check.

entries.name: Zealous Insight
entries: When you succeed on a {@action Search|XPHB} action's Wisdom ({@skill Insight|XPHB}) check to detect lies or evasive answers, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} rolls involving combat against that creature for 1 hour.

entities.name: Deathbound
entries: You have seen death many times, up close and personal, and sometimes at your own hand. These experiences have affected you deeply, teaching you about life and death. You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: One Last Breath
entries: If you have two {@variantrule Death Saving Throw|XPHB} failures, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on {@variantrule Death Saving Throw|XPHB|Death Saving Throws} until you are no longer at 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}.

entries.name: Recuperation
entries: When you spend a {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB|Hit Point Die} during a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} to recover {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, you can roll the {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB|Hit Point Die} twice and use the higher roll.

entities.name: Dual Shot
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Dual Shot
entries: When you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your turn and attack with a bow or crossbow, you can make one extra attack as part of the same action against a creature that is within 10 feet of the original target that is within the weapon's range. If you do so, both attacks are made with {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB}.

entities.name: Expanded Grip
entries: Your natural ability with larger weapons lets you use them in a way others can't. You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: One-Handed Grip
entries: When you use a {@itemProperty V|XPHB|Versatile} weapon with one hand, the weapon deals the damage in parentheses when used to make a melee attack.

entries.name: Zealous Grasp
entries: If an effect forces you to drop what you are holding, you can make a DC 15 Strength saving throw to maintain your grip. When you are subjected to an effect that allows you to make a saving throw to maintain your grip, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the saving throw.

entities.name: Flurry
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Quick Strike
entries: Once on each of your turns when you make an attack roll with a weapon or an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} and have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the roll, you can forgo {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on that attack roll. After resolving that attack, you can then make another attack with the same weapon or {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} against a different creature. The new target must be within 5 feet of the first target and within the weapon's reach.

entities.name: Fortune of the Thaumaturge
entries: Your connection to the Thaumaturge puts you in touch with forces outside mortal understanding, making others think you were born under a fortunate moon. You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Fortune's Fortitude
entries: When you fail a {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB}, you can expend and roll one {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB|Hit Point Die}, adding that number to your result. You can do this only once per {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB}.
You can use this benefit a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, and you regain all uses of this ability when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Fortune of the Unknown
entries: When using the Fortune's Fortitude benefit, if you roll the highest or lowest number on the {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB|Hit Point Die}, you regain that {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB|Hit Point Die}. It still counts as a use of Fortune's Fortitude.

entities.name: Free Sword Mercenary's Will
entries: Your training with the Company of Free Swords has given you an increased stamina and an attitude that make you less likely to succumb to hardships. You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Hold the Ground
entries: When you are moved without using your movement by a creature, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to reduce the distance you are moved by up to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}.

entries.name: Resolute
entries: You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws that would apply a condition.

entities.name: Hulking Figure
entries: Either from extensive training or a natural build, you have a broad and formidable size for your species. You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Brutal
entries: Once per turn, you can deal an extra {@damage 1d4} Bludgeoning damage to a target you hit with an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}.

entries.name: Intimidating
entries: When you make a Charisma ({@skill Intimidation|XPHB}, {@skill Performance|XPHB}, or {@skill Persuasion|XPHB}) check, you can also add your Strength modifier to the roll.

entries.name: Powerful
entries: You count as one size larger (to a maximum size of Large) when determining your carrying capacity.

entities.name: Insightful Collector
entries: Your expeditions, transactions, and mercantile activities on behalf of the Augustine Trading Company have put you in contact with collectors of historical artifacts, relics, and tomes. You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Object Intuition
entries: You can take a {@action Study|XPHB} action to examine one magical object and learn its properties and how to use it without attuning to it or taking a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} in physical contact with it, but you don't learn any curse the item might bear. There is a cumulative 5% chance for every rarity above Common that you misidentify the nature and the power of the object.

entries.name: Rare Find
entries: You begin play in possession of a Common magic item. Work with your GM to decide on which object fits your character and the campaign.

entities.name: Iron Gut
entries: You can eat like a hill giant and drink like a fish. Years of punishing your stomach and liver have led to a powerful fortitude. You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Inured to Poison
entries: You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws you make to avoid or end the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition.

entries.name: Everything Looks Delicious
entries: You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Wisdom ({@skill Survival|XPHB}) checks to forage for food.

entries.name: Quick to Recover
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can expend one of your {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB}, roll the die and add your Constitution modifier, and regain a number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the roll's total. Once you use this benefit, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Lightning Caster
entries: Your rapid-fire style of casting cantrips allows you to weave magic with uncanny speed. You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Dual Target
entries: When you cast a cantrip with a casting time of an action that targets a single creature, you can use a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to target a second creature within the cantrip's range.

entries.name: Immediate Response
entries: When you cast a spell as a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}, that spell doesn't expend a spell slot. Once you use this benefit, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Medician of the Morbus Doctore
entries: You are a master of the medical sciences because of your association with a medical institute. You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Adept Medic
entries: When you use the Blood and Bone benefit of the Triage Expert feat, the creature can spend up to three {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB} instead of one.

entries.name: Battle Surgeon
entries: When you use the Blood and Bone benefit of the Triage Expert feat, the creature can spend three {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB} to end one of the following conditions on itself instead of regaining {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}: {@condition Blinded|XPHB}, {@condition Deafened|XPHB}, {@condition Paralyzed|XPHB}, {@condition Poisoned|XPHB}, or {@condition Stunned|XPHB}.
Alternatively, you can cure one {@variantrule Grievous Wounds|GrimHollowCG24|Grievous Wound} affecting the creature.

entities.name: Mobile Combatant
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Slippery
entries: When you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action, your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} increases by 10 feet and {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks} have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} against you until the end of your turn.

entities.name: Nimble Physique
entries: You are small and thin for your species. You have a mysterious and consistent ability to avoid danger. You gain the following benefits:

items.name: Dodgy
entries: While you aren't wearing armor or wielding a {@item Shield|XPHB}, you can take the {@action Dodge|XPHB} action as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}.

items.name: Slippery
entries: While you are {@condition Grappled|XPHB} or {@condition Restrained|XPHB}, your attacks don't have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} and attacks against you don't have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB}.

entities.name: Opportunist
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Exploit Weakness
entries: Whenever you make an attack as part of a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}, you gain a +2 bonus to the attack and damage rolls.

entities.name: Prone Defense
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Defensive
entries: When you have the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition, you don't have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on attack rolls. {@action Attack|XPHB} rolls against you don't have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} because of the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition.

entries.name: Hop Up
entries: When you have the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition, you can right yourself with only 5 feet of movement.

entities.name: Resolution of the Syndicate
entries: Membership in the Ebon Syndicate provides perks—one of which is the motivation that comes with the understanding that the Syndicate kills those who fail too often. You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Quick Strike
entries: On your first turn after an {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} roll, add {@dice 1d4} to the first damage roll you make. This extra damage increases to {@dice 2d4} at character level 9, and to {@dice 4d4} at character level 16.

entries.name: Resilient
entries: Your {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} maximum increases by an amount equal to your character level when you gain this feat. Whenever you gain a character level thereafter, your {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} maximum increases by an additional 1 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point}.

entities.name: Sangromantic Initiate
entries: You have become adept enough with weaving blood magic to mitigate some of the harm of Sangromancy. You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Blood Magic
entries: Choose a {@filter Sangromancy spell|spells|subschool=sangromancy} from any spell list of a level for which you have spell slots. You always have that spell prepared. You can cast it once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it in that way when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also cast the spell using any spell slot you have.

entries.name: Sanguine Potency
entries: You have a pool of two d12s that you can expend instead of {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB} when you cast {@filter Sangromancy spells|spells|subschool=sangromancy}. Your pool regains all expended dice when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entities.name: Shadowsteel Adept
entries: You have learned to tap into the dangerous but powerful art of Shadowsteel casting. You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Curse Caster
entries: You always have {@filter Shadowsteel curses|spells|subschool=shadowsteel curse} prepared, and you can cast them with any spell slots you have.

entries.name: Shadowsteel's Bite
entries: While holding your {@item Shadowsteel Focus|GrimHollowPG24}, you gain a +1 bonus to spell attack rolls and to the saving throw DCs of your spells.

entries.name: Shadowsteel Weapon
entries: If your {@item Shadowsteel Focus|GrimHollowPG24} is also a weapon, that weapon has a +1 bonus to weapon attack rolls and damage rolls.

entities.name: Shadowsteel Master
entries: Your mastery over Shadowsteel grows stronger. You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Necrotic Harmony
entries: While holding your {@item Shadowsteel Focus|GrimHollowPG24}, when you cast a spell that targets one or more creatures and requires an attack roll or saving throw, you can choose one target you hit or who failed the saving throw to also take {@damage 1d4} Necrotic damage for each spell slot level expended to cast the spell.

entries.name: Necrotic Weapon
entries: If your {@item Shadowsteel Focus|GrimHollowPG24} is also a weapon, that weapon has a +2 bonus to weapon attack rolls and damage rolls. Additionally, once on each of your turns, when you hit a creature with a weapon attack roll using your {@item Shadowsteel Focus|GrimHollowPG24}, you can cause the target to take an extra {@damage 2d8} Necrotic damage.

entities.name: Survivor
entries: Your ability to make the best out of a bad situation has served you well. You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Hardy
entries: You need half the amount of food per day based on your size.

entries.name: Intuitive
entries: When you take the {@action Study|XPHB} action, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Intelligence checks made when taking the {@action Study|XPHB} action.

entries.name: Shake It Off
entries: Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB}, your {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} level, if any, decreases by 1. Additionally, when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, your {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} level decreases by 2.

entities.name: Syndicate Spy
entries: Rising in the ranks of the Ebon Syndicate has taught you the clandestine skills of spycraft, valuable in your trade as a racketeer. You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Locksmith
entries: You have proficiency with {@item Thieves' Tools|XPHB}. While holding {@item Thieves' Tools|XPHB}, you can craft a key to a lock by spending 10 minutes with that lock.

entries.name: Master of Disguise
entries: You have proficiency with a {@item Disguise Kit|XPHB}. If you spend 10 minutes donning a {@item Costume|XPHB},  you decide what you look like, including your height, weight, facial features, sound of your voice, hair length, coloration, and other distinguishing characteristics. You can make yourself appear as a member of another species, though none of your statistics change. You can't appear as a creature of a different size, and your basic shape stays the same; if you're bipedal, you can't use this benefit to become quadrupedal, for instance.

entries.name: Master Calligrapher
entries: You have proficiency with a {@item Forgery Kit|XPHB}. You aren't limited to 10 or fewer words when mimicking someone else's handwriting.

entries.name: Blending In
entries: When using the {@action Hide|XPHB} action to conceal yourself from creatures that aren't {@variantrule Hostile [Attitude]|XPHB|Hostile} to you, you need only be {@variantrule Lightly Obscured|XPHB} and you may use Charisma instead of Dexterity for the check.

entities.name: Thrown Weapon Master
entries: You excel at thrown weapons. You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Multithrow
entries: After you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action to make a ranged attack roll using a Simple weapon that has {@itemProperty T|XPHB|Thrown}, you can make two additional ranged attacks using Simple weapons that have {@itemProperty T|XPHB|Thrown} as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}.

entries.name: Quick Hands
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can retrieve and stow or wield all Simple weapons with {@itemProperty T|XPHB|Thrown} that are within 5 feet of you. You need a free hand to perform this maneuver.

entries.name: Returning
entries: Simple weapons with {@itemProperty T|XPHB|Thrown} that you are proficient with also have the {@itemMastery Returning|GrimHollowPG24} mastery property.

entities.name: Triage Expert
entries: You are trained in dealing with injuries on the battlefield, and with the saving of lives afterward. You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Bedside Manner
entries: Whenever you cause a creature to regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} or the Blood and Bone benefit, you can roll one extra die and discard the lowest die.

entries.name: Blood and Bone
entries: Taking the {@action Utilize|XPHB} action and expending the use of a {@item Healer's Kit|XPHB} lets you heal a creature within 5 feet of you. The creature can expend and roll a {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB|Hit Point Die} and regain that number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}.

entities.name: Witch Hunter
entries: You have honed and perfected your skills fighting against spellcasters, possibly from your background hunting mages as part of the Arcanist Inquisition. You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Dodge Spells
entries: You can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to avoid a spell that is targeting only you and doesn't create an area of effect. Make a Wisdom saving throw against the spellcaster's spell save DC. On a successful save, the creature must choose a new target or the spell is canceled. A canceled spell dissipates with no effect, and any resources used to cast it are wasted.

entries.name: Keep Your Enemies Close
entries: When you hit a target that can cast spells with an attack roll using a Melee weapon or an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, you can reduce its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} by 15 feet until the start of your next turn.

entries.name: Resist Curses
entries: You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against all Shadowsteel curses, and also against spells with a duration of more than 10 minutes.

entities.name: Adroit Crafter

otherSummary.entry: Proficiency in a skill or tool

entries: You've trained in crafting items using a skill or tool that you're proficient with, and the steps in crafting have become second nature to you. You gain the following benefits:

items: The time it takes to create an item using your proficient skills or tools is halved.
If you fail to successfully craft the item and would lose the components, you retain the components instead.

entities.name: Careful Crafter

otherSummary.entry: Proficiency in a skill or tool

entries: You've trained in crafting items using a skill or tool that you're proficient with, and your creations are known for being well made and reliable. You gain the following benefits:

items: You make all ability checks for crafting with {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB}.
Anyone assisting you in crafting an item gains {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on their ability checks for crafting.
If you fail to successfully craft the item, the amount of time it took to complete the process is halved.
